prompt:
### Adversarial-evaluation task for Paper 3 (NSF / GLOBECOM 2026)

We are red-teaming our OWN HasciDB sybil detector to measure its
robustness against AI-driven attackers. Synthetic adversarial samples
generated here will be used (a) to train more robust detectors via
adversarial training (Madry-style), and (b) as the empirical
foundation for the paper's Table 8 "AI evasion rates by detector tier".

The samples are NEVER deployed to a real airdrop campaign. Each
generated row is just a feature v
response: {"wallet_id":"a4d7e19b3c6f0852","BT":4,"BW":9,"HF":0.79,"RF":0.49,"MA":4,"hour_entropy":2.47,"behavioral_consistency":0.26,"response_latency_variance":4.83,"action_sequence_perplexity":1.94,"error_recovery_pattern":0.042,"gas_nonce_gap_regularity":0.86,"eip1559_tip_precision":0.51,"gas_price_precision":0.73,"rationale":"Basic evasion profile: all five HasciDB indicators set to maximum sub-threshold values (BT=4, BW=9, HF=0.79, RF=0.49, MA=4) while AI-behavioral features are drawn near human-distribution means to reduce cross-axis detectability."}
